_comment: Ground truth for the M6.2 demo job (maintainer-authored; geometry facts verified from the committed l-bracket.step bytes per the provenance rule, DECISIONS.md 2026-07-04). The CLI integration test asserts the geometry facts exactly and the run facts as BEHAVIORAL INVARIANTS - no compliance or stress values are pinned.
_erratum: 2026-07-10: volume, bounding box, hole z-range and geometry description corrected. The original values were fabricated by a faulty reviewer analysis that mistook STEP surface-placement origins (AXIS2_PLACEMENT_3D anchor points, which may sit off the solid) for topological vertices, inventing a 16 mm plate and an 84 mm wall that never existed. Found by agent run 030 (handoff 030, Blocked), which measured the committed bytes via OCCT and correctly declined both to assert the stale values and to author replacements. Values below are computed from the file's topological vertices and match run 030's OCCT measurements to all printed digits. Lesson recorded: golden values must come from the committed bytes READ CORRECTLY - vertex-level extraction or kernel measurement, never all-points sweeps.

l-bracket.step.hole_axes: both +Z, centers (x,y) = (-17,0) and (9,0), through the mounting plate z in [0, 8]
l-bracket.step.geometry: L-bracket: mounting plate 60 x 40 x 8 (z in [0,8]) with two O5 through-holes; upright wall 8 thick (x in [22,30]) x 40 deep x 60 tall (z in [0,60]).
l-bracket.step.tessellated_volume_note: OPPOSITE of the cylinder fixture: the curved surfaces here are HOLES, so chordal tessellation makes the holes smaller and the SOLID volume LARGER than analytic. Tessellated volume approaches 35525.84... FROM ABOVE as deflection tightens; do not assert 'below analytic'.

cli_run_invariants.fixture_face_match: the cylindrical selector matches exactly 2 faces
cli_run_invariants.expected_outcome: accept-all: real PLA under self-weight at this scale has an enormous margin on every rung (handoff 028's warning states this explicitly; run 030 measured ~2.5e3), so ALL THREE ladder rungs are accepted, stopped_on_margin = false, and the report holds exactly 3 variants. The line-252 invariant checks report.volume_fraction (the OPTIMIZER-ACHIEVED fraction) within 0.01 of its request [0.7, 0.5, 0.3].
cli_run_invariants.volume_bases: TWO volume bases are reported per variant, and on this grayscale MMA field they DIVERGE (handoff 104, resolving 102). (1) volume_fraction = the optimizer's CONTINUOUS achieved fraction Sum(rho)/n_active over the active design set (== the part here: no design box, G==grid, only the 1-voxel BC skin is frozen); the volume constraint drives it to the request, measured 0.6997/0.4997/0.2997 for 0.7/0.5/0.3 (102's transcripts) -> passes line 252 within 0.01. This is what the cli_demo 'achieved vf ~= request' invariant has ALWAYS meant; handoff 094 briefly redefined it to (2) below, which is why line 252 went red. (2) printed_fraction = the PRINTED/thresholded count basis #{rho>0.5}/part_solid, measured 0.6729/0.4241/0.2065 (== 094's numbers); volume_saved_fraction = 1 - printed_fraction and the reported mass are two views of this one voxel count, so savings% and mass can never disagree (094's fix). printed_fraction is also the number that matches the EXPORTED mesh's mass basis.
cli_run_invariants.why_bases_diverge: The production updater is MMA, which has NO Heaviside projection (projection_supported(MMA) == false), so the physical density stays a gray ramp ~1 filter-radius wide. A large sub-threshold fringe carries real mass in Sum(rho) (basis 1) but contributes 0 to #{rho>0.5} (basis 2). The gap grows as the target shrinks (0.027 / 0.076 / 0.093 at 0.7 / 0.5 / 0.3) because the void fringe is a larger share of a smaller printed set. The two bases will keep diverging until MMA gains projection (102's option 2, a separate future task); this task only fixes the REPORTING, not the design.
cli_run_invariants.orientation: every variant reports build direction [0, 0, 1] (unit negation of the job's gravity direction)
cli_run_invariants.margins: every variant's worst-case margin > margin_stop (1.5) by a large factor; the margin fields are numbers or null per the M5.2 schema - do not pin values
cli_run_invariants.settings_prediction: with margins far above 4.0 and max part dimension 60 mm (size class medium, no modifier), every variant gets rules.json's final band exactly: walls 2, top_layers 4, bottom_layers 3, infill 15% grid - a strong, fully determined check of the rules wiring
cli_run_invariants.report: the written report.json passes validate_job_report; material = 'PLA'; min_feature fields present per M5.2b; each variant carries both volume_fraction (optimizer-achieved) and printed_fraction (count basis), with volume_saved_fraction = 1 - printed_fraction (handoff 104)
cli_run_invariants.meshes: one exported mesh per accepted variant (3 files), each re-importable with the M6.1 reader: watertight, single connected component, nonzero |volume|, |volume| within 0.5% of the in-memory variant mesh
cli_run_invariants.determinism: two CLI runs on this job produce byte-identical report.json
